title: Silky Saudi-Arabian Comte Pilaf
description: A rich and creamy pilaf with a hint of wine, inspired by the flavors of Saudi-Arabia. Perfect for a comforting dinner or a special occasion.
instructions: Heat the ghee or neutral oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the garlic and ginger and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the carrots and celery and sauté for 5 minutes until tender.
Add the chickpeas, chicken broth, and Marsala wine. Bring to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Stir in the Comte cheese until melted and smooth.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes until the cheese is fully incorporated and the pilaf is heated through.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
Transfer to a serving dish and serve immediately.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
